# ANALYSIS: ORB_GATE_COMPARISON_Q2_2026

**Filed:** 2026-06-18
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** walk-forward analysis
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-MORE-DATA)

## Hypothesis

ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE (MAX_OR_RANGE=2.00 + long-only) is more regime-robust than ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER (long-only only) because the OR-range filter reduces exposure to noisy, wide-opening-range days, leading to more stable win rates and Sharpe ratios across varying market regimes. The direction filter alone may capture strong trending periods but suffers from regime concentration.

## Mechanism

- **ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE**: Entry on ORB retest long signals only when the 30-minute opening range (ORH-ORL) is < $2.00 and the watcher confidence is medium. Exit uses chart stop at ORH with premium stop = -0.99 (chart-stop-only). No engine feature-add needed as OR range is computed internally.
- **ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER**: Entry on all ORB retest long signals regardless of OR range, with same exit rules. Watcher filters out short signals.

## Data availability

Quarterly P&L and trade counts are available from the leaderboard and candidate files, but monthly breakdown for Q2 2026 (April, May, June) is not provided in the inputs. Walk-forward analysis requiring monthly windows cannot be completed without Stage-1 backtest that outputs per-month equity curves.

## Expected impact on regime robustness (based on quarterly proxies)

| Gate | Q2 2026 trades | Q2 2026 P&L | Q2 2026 WR | Q1 2026 P&L | Q1 2026 WR | P&L stability (Q1 vs Q2) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE | 5 | +$155 | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | 7 | +$366 | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest |
| ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER | 133 | +$6,245 | 86% | 13 | -$110 | 62% | large swing from loss to gain |

The quarterly data show ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER is heavily concentrated in Q2 2026 (≈85% of total P&L), while ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE shows more balanced contributions across Q1 and Q2. However, without monthly granularity we cannot compute OOS/IS Sharpe ratios or win-rate stability via walk-forward.

## Conclusion

ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE appears more regime-robust based on quarterly P&L distribution, but a definitive walk-forward analysis using 2026 Q2 monthly windows requires additional backtest. Recommend running a Stage-1 backtest that exports monthly equity curves for both gates, then compute IS/OOS Sharpe and WR ratios per rolling window.

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- analysis limited by missing monthly data; quarterly proxies suggest ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE is more stable but cannot confirm.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This analysis does not propose a new candidate; it informs the relative merits of ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE (rank 4, PROMISING) and ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER (rank 5, NEEDS-MORE-DATA). No conflict with existing leaderboard entries.
